compliance_evidence.business_summary: The business objective is to operate an insurance data pipeline that processes customer, policy, premium, billing, and claims data for operations in India and the United States. The compliance scope covers all ingested data related to insurance customers, policies, premiums, billing, and claims, with a requirement to identify and protect personal information, government identifiers, financial values, health and claim details, contact information, and regulated insurance attributes. The platform processes a wide range of enterprise data, including direct and indirect PII, government IDs (Aadhaar, PAN, SSN), financial and health information, and contact details. The overall compliance assessment demonstrates that the platform has identified relevant PII and sensitive data, mapped findings to regulatory obligations, and recommended appropriate security controls for each data element, supporting business and regulatory requirements prior to code generation.
compliance_evidence.regulatory_summary: The applicable regulations include the NAIC Privacy Statement, GLBA Title V, Insurance Information and Privacy Protection Model Act, Health Information Privacy Model Act, HIPAA Privacy Rule, and Model Privacy Regulation. Major regulatory obligations identified are: collection, use, and disclosure of personal and financial information for specified business purposes; providing correction and opt-out procedures; obtaining affirmative consent before disclosing personal or health information; annual privacy notices; and strict limitations on sharing nonpublic personal information. Overall compliance considerations require pseudonymization and controlled recovery of sensitive data, with particular attention to government identifiers and health information. Significant compliance risks are associated with improper handling of government IDs (Aadhaar, PAN, SSN), financial data, and health information, which are subject to critical regulatory obligations and require affirmative consent and strong privacy protections.
compliance_evidence.security_summary: The recommended security controls are primarily pseudonymization for direct and indirect PII, government identifiers, financial data, health information, and other sensitive attributes. Pseudonymization is applied to customer names, contact details, government IDs (Aadhaar, PAN, SSN), financial values (bank account, card last four, payment method, premium, claim amounts), health information (diagnosis code, medical history), and other identifiers (policy, claim, billing, underwriting). No additional controls are recommended for data classified as Internal or not highly sensitive; standard database access controls are deemed sufficient for these. The overall security posture is robust for regulated and sensitive data, with pseudonymization supporting privacy, regulatory reporting, and business analytics, while maintaining compliance with identified obligations.

column_evidence.security_rationale: Structured customer identifier used to join personal records. Not inherently sensitive but links to PII. column_evidence.column_description: Customer occupation used for insurance risk evaluation and underwriting classification.
column_evidence.detection_reason: Column name, description, and sample value indicate occupation, which is personal information and may be sensitive in context.
column_evidence.classification_justification: Occupation is personal information, not highly sensitive but used for risk evaluation. Internal use only.
column_evidence.security_rationale: Occupation is personal information but classified as Internal and used for risk evaluation. No regulatory obligation for additional technical protection. Standard database access controls are sufficient.

column_evidence.column_description: Customer annual income used for underwriting eligibility and financial risk assessment.
column_evidence.detection_reason: Column name, description, tags, and sample value indicate annual income, which is sensitive financial information.
column_evidence.classification_justification: Annual income is sensitive financial information relevant to underwriting and risk assessment.
column_evidence.security_rationale: Sensitive financial information (annual income) classified as Confidential. Pseudonymization allows controlled recovery for business analytics and regulatory reporting while protecting customer privacy.

column_evidence.column_description: Timestamp when the source record was created for auditability and pipeline lineage tracking.
column_evidence.detection_reason: Column name, description, and sample value indicate a system timestamp for audit tracking, not PII.
column_evidence.classification_justification: Timestamp for audit tracking and pipeline lineage. Not PII or sensitive business information.
column_evidence.security_rationale: System timestamp for audit tracking. Not PII or sensitive business information. No regulatory obligation for additional technical protection. Standard database access controls are sufficient.

column_evidence.column_description: Timestamp when the source record was last updated for auditability and change tracking.
column_evidence.detection_reason: Column name, description, and sample value indicate a system timestamp for audit tracking, not PII.
column_evidence.classification_justification: Timestamp for audit tracking and change lineage. Not PII or sensitive business information.
column_evidence.security_rationale: System timestamp for audit tracking. Not PII or sensitive business information. No regulatory obligation for additional technical protection. Standard database access controls are sufficient.
